meta.note: validated at 537226e0c7be97d19517385e35523d0b6158c5c4; deep-corrected evidence for config.py/main.py/scanner.py/storage.py-derived concepts (drifted line ranges from AcmeConfig/SpiffeConfig split + CLI additions), fast-path bumped last_validated for concepts whose module had zero commits since prior extraction commit 4d79b09a1a0699e9807113c883511f6001034b73

cert-issuance-mode-strict-exclusivity.description: CERT_ISSUANCE_MODE dispatches construction to exactly one of two separate pydantic classes, AcmeConfig or SpiffeConfig, via the make_settings() factory. The inactive mode's fields do not exist on the constructed object at all (no coercion/defaulting) -- accessing them raises AttributeError. Stray env vars for the inactive mode's fields are logged as a WARNING from inside make_settings().
invariants: CERT_ISSUANCE_MODE is either 'acme' or 'spiffe', never both active
Exactly one of AcmeConfig/SpiffeConfig is ever instantiated per process (config.settings)
config.settings has CA_PROVIDER/MANAGED_DOMAINS/etc only when it is an AcmeConfig, and SPIRE_AGENT_SOCKET_PATH/SPIFFE_TRUST_DOMAIN/MANAGED_SPIFFE_IDS only when it is a SpiffeConfig
SPIFFE_TRUST_DOMAIN is still a hard requirement (raises) when mode=spiffe -- no sensible default to invent
contracts: Construction-time validation rejects a misconfigured mode before any network call
Code paths shared across both modes (agent/nodes/scanner.py, agent/nodes/storage.py) must read mode-specific fields via getattr(config.settings, field, default), never assume presence
cert-issuance-mode-strict-exclusivity.name: cert-issuance-mode-strict-exclusivity

csv-fallback-settings-source.description: Custom pydantic-settings sources catch JSON-parse failures on List[str] fields and pass the raw string through so field_validators can split on commas, supporting comma-separated env vars.
invariants: Only ValueError from prepare_field_value is swallowed, not other exceptions
contracts: MANAGED_DOMAINS and MANAGED_SPIFFE_IDS accept either JSON arrays or comma-separated strings from env/.env
csv-fallback-settings-source.name: csv-fallback-settings-source

eab-all-or-nothing-validation.description: For CA providers requiring External Account Binding (digicert, zerossl, sectigo), both ACME_EAB_KEY_ID and ACME_EAB_HMAC_KEY must be set together or neither.
invariants: key_set == hmac_set for EAB-required providers
contracts: Fails fast at settings construction rather than at first ACME network call
eab-all-or-nothing-validation.name: eab-all-or-nothing-validation

acme-directory-preset-resolution.description: Resolves ACME_DIRECTORY_URL from a CA_PROVIDER preset map unless CA_PROVIDER is 'custom', in which case ACME_DIRECTORY_URL must be explicitly provided; also warns on deprecated DIGICERT_* env vars.
invariants: ACME_DIRECTORY_URL is always resolved by the time log_resolved_settings runs (declaration order dependency)
contracts: Callers can rely on settings.ACME_DIRECTORY_URL being populated after Settings construction in acme mode
acme-directory-preset-resolution.name: acme-directory-preset-resolution

cli-readonly-query-commands.description: CLI entry point distinguishes state-changing commands (--once, --schedule, --revoke-cert) from strictly read-only query commands (--expiring-in-30-days, --domain-status) that never issue/renew/revoke certs.
invariants: --expiring-in-30-days and --domain-status never mutate cert store or contact ACME/SPIRE servers
contracts: Query commands are safe to call repeatedly with no side effects
cli-readonly-query-commands.name: cli-readonly-query-commands

cli-command-registry-dispatch.description: main() builds a dict mapping action name to (is_selected, handler) and executes the first matching handler, replacing an if/elif chain with a declarative registry.
invariants: Exactly one handler executes per invocation (first match wins per dict iteration order)
At least one action flag must be selected or help is printed and process exits 1
contracts: Adding a new CLI action requires registering it in command_registry
cli-command-registry-dispatch.name: cli-command-registry-dispatch

runtime-settings-override-singleton-swap.description: apply_runtime_settings_overrides replaces the module-level config.settings singleton with a new Settings object built from CLI overrides (--ca-provider/--acme-directory-url), affecting the whole process for that run.
invariants: Override only occurs if at least one of ca_provider/acme_directory_url is provided
contracts: Downstream code reading config.settings sees the overridden values for the remainder of the process
runtime-settings-override-singleton-swap.name: runtime-settings-override-singleton-swap

mcp-tool-serialization-lock.description: State-mutating MCP tools (renew_once, revoke_cert, generate_test_cert, health) acquire a process-wide asyncio.Lock via _operation_lock(required=True) to serialize execution against the shared config.settings singleton override mechanism; read-only tools use required=False and skip locking.
invariants: Only one mutating MCP tool call touches config.settings/global state at a time
Read-only tools never block on or require the lock
contracts: Concurrent MCP tool calls cannot interleave settings overrides mid-operation
mcp-tool-serialization-lock.name: mcp-tool-serialization-lock

mcp-temporary-settings-override-contextm.description: _temporary_settings_override swaps config.settings for the duration of a single tool call and restores the original in a finally block, enabling per-call CA overrides without permanently mutating global state (unlike main.py's CLI override).
invariants: config.settings is always restored to its prior value after the tool call, even on exception
contracts: Callers can pass per-request settings_override without affecting subsequent MCP calls
mcp-temporary-settings-override-contextm.name: mcp-temporary-settings-override-contextmanager

mcp-ca-input-mode-explicit-resolution.description: _resolve_ca_inputs requires explicit ca_input_mode ('config' or 'custom') rather than inferring from optional params, validating ca_provider against known choices and URL scheme when in 'custom' mode.
invariants: ca_input_mode must be 'config' or 'custom'
In 'custom' mode both ca_provider and acme_directory_url are required and validated
contracts: Tool callers get explicit ValueError rather than silently ignored/misapplied overrides
mcp-ca-input-mode-explicit-resolution.name: mcp-ca-input-mode-explicit-resolution

domain-path-traversal-guard.description: _validate_domain_for_cert_generation blocks path separators, '..' sequences, and leading dots before a domain string is used to build a filesystem path under CERT_STORE_PATH, in generate_test_cert.
invariants: domain never contains '/', '\\', '..' or starts with '.'
Certificate generation always writes under the configured CERT_STORE_PATH, never an arbitrary path
contracts: Callers cannot escape the cert store directory via crafted domain names
domain-path-traversal-guard.name: domain-path-traversal-guard

jsonl-structured-logging.description: JSONLFormatter emits one JSON object per log line including a run_id, merging any extra fields attached via logging's extra= kwarg or filters, with exceptions serialized as a single string field to preserve valid JSONL.
invariants: Every log line is valid, single-line JSON
Standard LogRecord attributes are excluded from the merged extras to avoid duplication
contracts: Callers can add structured fields via extra= and they will appear verbatim in output
jsonl-structured-logging.name: jsonl-structured-logging

logger-singleton-run-id.description: LoggerWithRunID is a process-wide singleton (via __new__ check) ensuring a single run_id is stamped on all log lines for the process lifetime, wrapping a LoggerDecorator that adds the RunIDFilter and JSONL handler.
invariants: Only one LoggerWithRunID instance exists per process
run_id is generated once at first construction and never changes
contracts: All modules importing `logger` from logger.py share the same run_id-tagged logger
logger-singleton-run-id.name: logger-singleton-run-id

graph-mode-branching-acme-vs-spiffe.description: build_graph() branches on settings.CERT_ISSUANCE_MODE to wire exactly one of two disjoint node sets/edge chains (_build_acme_graph or _build_spiffe_graph) into a shared StateGraph, sharing scanner/planner/pick_next_domain/storage_manager/summary_reporter but diverging on issuance mechanics.
invariants: Never both ACME and SPIFFE node sets are wired into the same compiled graph
Both modes share the same entry (certificate_scanner) and terminal (summary_reporter → END) nodes
contracts: initial_state() produces a state dict compatible with whichever graph is built, regardless of mode
graph-mode-branching-acme-vs-spiffe.name: graph-mode-branching-acme-vs-spiffe

spiffe-mode-terminal-failure-semantics.description: SPIFFE mode graph has no error_handler/retry_scheduler nodes — spiffe_attestor failures are terminal per-domain, handled the same way storage_manager already handles its own failures (recorded and loop continues via domain_loop_router).
invariants: SPIFFE mode never retries a failed attestation within the same domain iteration
Failures in spiffe_attestor must still allow domain_loop_router to proceed to the next domain
contracts: Callers of the SPIFFE graph should not expect retry/backoff behavior identical to ACME mode
spiffe-mode-terminal-failure-semantics.name: spiffe-mode-terminal-failure-semantics

acme-error-routing-triad.description: error_action_router reads the plain state field error_action (set directly by error_handler, not JSON-parsed) to route to retry/skip_domain/abort, defaulting to skip_domain on missing/unrecognized values, and further gates 'retry' on retry_count < max_retries.
invariants: error_action must never be re-parsed from error_analysis prose
retry is only honored when retry_count < max_retries even if action=='retry'
contracts: error_handler must set error_action to one of 'retry'|'skip'|'abort' before this router runs
acme-error-routing-triad.name: acme-error-routing-triad

pick-next-domain-state-reset.description: PickNextDomainNode pops the next domain from pending_renewals and resets per-domain transient state (current_order, current_nonce, retry_count, retry_delay_seconds) so each domain's ACME flow starts clean.
invariants: current_order/current_nonce/retry_count are always reset when a new domain is picked
contracts: Downstream nodes (order_initializer, spiffe_attestor) can assume current_order is None at the start of a domain's processing
pick-next-domain-state-reset.name: pick-next-domain-state-reset

ca-mismatch-advisory-detection.description: CertificateScannerNode only performs CA detection on existing certs when CA_PROVIDER is 'custom'; for named providers, config is treated as authoritative and detection is skipped, though a mismatch warning still normalizes letsencrypt/letsencrypt_staging as equivalent.
invariants: detected_ca_provider is None whenever CA_PROVIDER is a named (non-custom) provider
letsencrypt and letsencrypt_staging are treated as equivalent for mismatch comparison only
contracts: Renewal always uses the configured CA_PROVIDER regardless of what detection finds
ca-mismatch-advisory-detection.name: ca-mismatch-advisory-detection

planner-llm-output-domain-validation.description: _parse_and_validate strips markdown fences, drops non-string entries, filters out hallucinated domains not in managed_domains, and backfills any unclassified managed domain into 'routine', guaranteeing every managed domain appears in exactly one bucket.
invariants: Every managed domain appears in exactly one of urgent/routine/skip after validation
No domain outside managed_domains is ever added to pending_renewals from LLM output
contracts: Callers of renewal_planner can trust pending_renewals contains only valid managed domains, even under a malformed/hallucinating LLM
planner-llm-output-domain-validation.name: planner-llm-output-domain-validation

planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch.description: RenewalPlannerNode.run dispatches to LLM-based or deterministic planning based on settings.LLM_DISABLED, with the deterministic path ordering no-cert domains first then expiry-soon domains sorted ascending by days.
invariants: Deterministic mode never calls out to any LLM/network
contracts: pending_renewals is always populated in priority order regardless of which path is taken
planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch.name: planner-llm-vs-deterministic-dispatch

account-key-not-in-state.description: The ACME account private key is never stored in AgentState (to avoid leaking into LangSmith traces); only the account_key_path is kept in state, and the key is loaded from disk on demand by nodes that need it.
invariants: AgentState never contains raw private key material for the account key
contracts: Any node needing the account key must load it fresh from account_key_path
account-key-not-in-state.name: account-key-not-in-state

domain-privkey-never-in-state.description: CsrGeneratorNode writes the generated domain private key directly to disk (mode 0o600) and only stores the CSR DER (hex-encoded) in state; the private key itself never passes through AgentState/checkpoints.
invariants: Domain private key file permissions are always set to 0o600 immediately after write
Domain private key content never appears in AgentState
contracts: storage_manager can read privkey.pem from disk after CSR generation completes
domain-privkey-never-in-state.name: domain-privkey-never-in-state

challenge-type-mode-consistency.description: order_initializer selects http-01 or dns-01 challenge type based on settings.HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE, and only populates dns_txt_values when in dns mode, keeping AcmeOrder's parallel lists consistent across auth_urls/challenge_urls/auth_domains/dns_txt_values.
invariants: All AcmeOrder list fields (auth_urls, challenge_urls, challenge_tokens, key_authorizations) remain parallel/same-length for a given order
dns_txt_values is empty unless HTTP_CHALLENGE_MODE=='dns'
contracts: challenge_setup/challenge_verifier can safely zip these parallel lists
challenge-type-mode-consistency.name: challenge-type-mode-consistency

challenge-lifecycle-shared-module-state.description: ChallengeSetupNode and ChallengeVerifierNode share module-level singletons (_standalone_server, _dns_provider) across the setup→verify→cleanup lifecycle for a single domain's challenge, since standalone HTTP server and DNS provider connections must persist between graph node invocations.
invariants: _standalone_server/_dns_provider are only non-None while a challenge is actively being served for the current domain
_cleanup_challenge always tears down the mode-appropriate resource (server stop, webroot file removal, or DNS TXT deletion) before returning control
contracts: challenge_verifier assumes challenge_setup already started serving the first token for standalone mode
challenge-lifecycle-shared-module-state.name: challenge-lifecycle-shared-module-state

authorization-already-valid-skip.description: ChallengeVerifierNode checks each authorization's current status before responding to its challenge; if already 'valid' (server-side reuse across orders per RFC 8555 §7.5), it skips responding to avoid a 'malformed' error from re-responding to a valid challenge.
invariants: Never POST a challenge response for an authorization already in 'valid' status
contracts: Verifier is idempotent/safe to call even when the CA has reused a prior authorization
authorization-already-valid-skip.name: authorization-already-valid-skip

pem-chain-leaf-vs-intermediate-split.description: _split_pem_chain splits DigiCert's full PEM chain response on '-----END CERTIFICATE-----' boundaries, treating the first block as the leaf cert.pem and the rest as chain.pem.
invariants: The first PEM block in full_chain_pem is always the leaf certificate for the requested domain
contracts: storage_manager writes cert.pem, chain.pem, fullchain.pem, and metadata.json consistently for every successful renewal
pem-chain-leaf-vs-intermediate-split.name: pem-chain-leaf-vs-intermediate-split

error-handler-fatal-vs-retryable-classif.description: ErrorHandlerNode classifies errors as fatal (unauthorized, accountdoesnotexist, badkey, externalaccountrequired substrings) forcing abort regardless of retry_count, otherwise applies exponential backoff (capped 300s) until max_retries then skips.
invariants: Fatal error patterns always force action='abort' regardless of retry_count/max_retries
Backoff delay is capped at 300 seconds
abort clears all pending_renewals and marks them failed, stopping the whole run
contracts: error_action_router can trust error_action is always one of retry/skip/abort after this node runs
error-handler-fatal-vs-retryable-classif.name: error-handler-fatal-vs-retryable-classification

retry-scheduler-backoff-gate.description: RetrySchedulerNode synchronously sleeps until state['retry_not_before'] elapses before clearing it, decoupling error-decision (error_handler) from timing enforcement (retry_scheduler) in the graph.
invariants: retry_not_before is always cleared to None after this node runs, whether or not a wait occurred
contracts: pick_next_domain is only re-entered for a retry after the scheduled backoff has elapsed
retry-scheduler-backoff-gate.name: retry-scheduler-backoff-gate

revocation-graph-best-effort-looping.description: The revocation subgraph has no error_handler/retry — cert_revoker failures are logged into failed_revocations and revocation_loop_router unconditionally continues to the next domain, making revocation best-effort across a batch.
invariants: A single domain's revocation failure never halts processing of remaining revocation_targets
contracts: revocation_reporter always receives complete revoked_domains/failed_revocations lists after all targets are processed
revocation-graph-best-effort-looping.name: revocation-graph-best-effort-looping

claude-cli-llm-provider-no-key-required.description: make_llm() special-cases LLM_PROVIDER='claude_cli' to shell out to the `claude` binary (--safe-mode --tools none) reusing the caller's existing OAuth login, bypassing the langchain/API-key path entirely and requiring no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or optional extras.
invariants: claude_cli path never requires langchain to be installed or any API key
--tools none and --safe-mode are always passed to prevent agentic/tool execution and reduce token overhead
contracts: ClaudeCLIChatModel.invoke() returns an object with a .content attribute compatible with langchain's AIMessage interface
claude-cli-llm-provider-no-key-required.name: claude-cli-llm-provider-no-key-required

node-registry-class-instantiation-factor.description: get_node(name) looks up a callable class in NODE_REGISTRY and instantiates it, enforcing that registry entries are classes (not pre-built instances) via an explicit TypeError check.
invariants: Every value in NODE_REGISTRY must be a class (isinstance(..., type)), never a bare function or instance
contracts: build_graph()/build_revocation_graph() can call get_node(name) for any registered name and get a fresh callable node instance
node-registry-class-instantiation-factor.name: node-registry-class-instantiation-factory

agent-state-typed-schema.description: AgentState is a single shared TypedDict spanning both the renewal graph and revocation graph, with revocation-specific fields (revocation_targets, current_revocation_domain, etc.) coexisting alongside renewal fields even though a given graph only uses one subset.
invariants: messages uses add_messages reducer for LangGraph message accumulation
acme_account_key is never a field in this TypedDict (kept out of state deliberately)
contracts: Both build_graph() and build_revocation_graph() construct/consume the same AgentState shape, differing only in which fields they populate
agent-state-typed-schema.name: agent-state-typed-schema
